title: Miso Butter Roast Chicken & Radishes
summary: A rich roast chicken dish with caramelised radishes and mushrooms, finished with a luscious miso butter sauce. Perfect for a comforting yet classy meal.

method.description: Preheat the oven to 200°C fan. Make sure your butter is at room temperature.

Generously season the chicken thighs with salt and black pepper. Place them skin-side down into an ovenproof frying pan over medium heat. Cook undisturbed for 8-10 minutes until the skin is golden brown. Transfer to a plate, skin-side up.
In the same pan, add the halved radishes and mushrooms sliced into 1cm-sized chunks. Stir-fry for 4-5 minutes until golden brown.
Pour in the chicken stock and rice vinegar, bring to a boil, then place the chicken skin-side up back on top.
Transfer the pan to the oven and roast for 25-30 minutes until the chicken is fully cooked.
Reheat the rice and divide it into serving boxes.
Once the chicken is cooked, place it on top of the rice with the roasted vegetables.
Whisk the miso, mirin, butter, and half the sesame seeds into the pan juices. Drizzle over the chicken and rice.
Finish with the remaining sesame seeds and serve.

tips: Storage: Store in airtight containers in the fridge for up to 3 days. Reheat before serving.
Freezing: Freeze in portions for up to 1 month. Defrost in the fridge overnight before reheating.
Substitutions: Swap chicken thighs for drumsticks or bone-in chicken breast. Use spinach instead of radishes for a different vegetable profile.
Serving Ideas: Pair with a side of pickled cucumbers or steamed greens for a balanced meal.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
